documents.title: Boston College public AI posture sources

ai_strategy.summary: BC combines a Generative AI @ BC hub, protected Copilot/Gemini/NotebookLM access, steering committees, research-use guidance, and school-level pilots rather than a single top-down AI strategy.

key_provisions: Use institutionally reviewed AI tools for sensitive university work.
Protect restricted, confidential, student, personnel, and research data when using AI tools.
Verify AI output and keep humans accountable for academic, administrative, and research decisions.
ai_policy.summary: Boston College's public AI policy posture is cautious and emphasizes responsible use, privacy, and human accountability based on reviewed sources.

bodies.name: Boston College AI governance and support functions
bodies.scope: BC combines a Generative AI @ BC hub, protected Copilot/Gemini/NotebookLM access, steering committees, research-use guidance, and school-level pilots rather than a single top-down AI strategy.

governance_structure.summary: Governance appears distributed across AI-facing offices, centers, technology units, teaching units, or committees; no standalone chief AI officer was confirmed unless named in leadership.

initiatives.description: Boston College has public AI guidance, governance, or strategy resources that frame responsible adoption, privacy, academic integrity, and human oversight.
initiatives.significance: This is a concrete R1 signal of AI moving from isolated experimentation toward governed, mission-linked adoption.

initiatives.title: Andromeda HPC upgrade for AI and LLM research
initiatives.description: ITS completed a 2025 Andromeda HPC upgrade that doubled GPU resources, added H200 capacity, migrated 900 TB of data, and supports about 400 BC researchers.
initiatives.significance: This closes Boston College’s compute-capacity and usage gap with named cluster, GPU count, and user community evidence.

investment.description: No public project cost was disclosed in the source.

collaborations.description: Internal ITS and faculty research-computing collaboration.

initiatives.title: AI integration in social work education
initiatives.description: Boston College School of Social Work describes AI integration into social work education through classroom, workforce, and practice-oriented preparation.
initiatives.significance: This gives a concrete curriculum and workforce signal beyond general campus AI tooling.

investment.description: No public dollar amount found in reviewed sources.

initiatives.policy_implications: Links AI use to professional education and AI-era practice readiness.

computing_infrastructure.summary: Boston College’s Andromeda HPC cluster underwent a 2025 overhaul and expansion, including doubled GPU resources and an H200 node for AI/LLM research. The public compute table lists 109 GPUs across A100, H200, RTX A6000, RTX 3090, RTX 6000 Ada, V100, L4, and V100S nodes.

regulatory_context.summary: State/system context was checked for MA. Relevant public AI or system governance links are included where found.

narrative_summary: Boston College is an R1 institution whose public AI posture is best understood as follows: BC combines a Generative AI @ BC hub, protected Copilot/Gemini/NotebookLM access, steering committees, research-use guidance, and school-level pilots rather than a single top-down AI strategy. The evidence touches governance and policy, teaching and learning, research and innovation, enterprise enablement, student experience, workforce development, and equity or public-impact dimensions. The strongest public sources are first-party AI hubs, guidance pages, research center pages, academic-program materials, and state or system policy resources. Compared with the most centralized AI adopters, several details remain opaque, including named executive AI ownership, exact enterprise user counts, and total AI spending. Even so, the reviewed public record supports a medium-confidence assessment that AI is moving into curriculum, research infrastructure, technology governance, and student or workforce-facing programs rather than remaining purely ad hoc.

gaps_acknowledged: No public chief AI officer was confirmed in reviewed sources.
No total institution-wide AI investment amount was found.
Exact enterprise AI user counts or contract values were usually not public.
Boston College now has public HPC usage and GPU evidence, but exact Copilot/Gemini/NotebookLM seat counts and contract values remain undisclosed.
Worker 3 did not find public institution-wide AI outcome metrics beyond the Andromeda researcher-count and migration details.

strategic_archetype.rationale: Primary archetype selected from visible public signals: balanced enterprise.
